description.current: Run before validation to detect missing dependencies, dead servers, unseeded databases. Auto-fixes common failures, produces CLEAR/BLOCKED/WARN verdict. Prevents mid-validation debugging cycles.
issues: Slightly short (25 words) but punchy and result-focused
Strong rationale: prevents debugging cycles mid-validation

issues.description: Relies heavily on reference files (platform-checklists.md, auto-fix-actions.md) but provides minimal inline guidance. Lines 21-32 defer all specifics to references. Body doesn't explain HOW to detect or fix.
Preflight Report Format template (lines 35-55) shows desired output but doesn't explain how to generate it. No mention of what tool/script produces this report.
Auto-fix logic (lines 24) says 'attempt one auto-fix per failed check, re-check after' but provides no examples of what auto-fixes are. Users left guessing.
Rules section (lines 66-73) includes 'NEVER auto-fix by installing major tools (Xcode, Docker)' but doesn't explain detection logic for 'major' vs 'minor' auto-fixes.

strengths: Severity Levels table (lines 58-64) is clear and actionable
Purpose statement (lines 14-18) is compelling: '10-30 minutes of debugging is expensive, failed preflight is cheaper'
Related Skills section (lines 81-86) shows good integration points
Security Policy (lines 75-79) is transparent about scope (diagnostic commands, service starts, no code modification)
Bottom-up validation order for fullstack (line 73) is crucial insight

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: No standalone preflight script that can be executed before validation. A run-preflight.sh that detects platform, runs platform-specific checks, auto-fixes, and produces the preflight report would be valuable executable reference.

top_fixes: Add 3-5 concrete inline examples of platform detection (grepping for package.json, xcodeproj, Cargo.toml) and corresponding checklist invocation
Provide examples of auto-fixes: 'npm install', 'docker run postgres', 'brew services start postgresql', 'xcode-select --install'. Show what is/isn't attempted.
Add a worked example: 'Web app preflight: Node check PASS, dependencies FAIL → auto-fix: npm install → re-check PASS, Dev server FAIL → auto-fix: npm run dev → re-check PASS. Report: CLEAR.'
Bundle run-preflight.sh script that orchestrates platform detection, checklist execution, and report generation. Make it executable standalone.
Clarify 'major tools': Xcode, Docker, Android SDK are skipped; npm install, database start, env var checks are auto-fixed

test_prompts.prompt: I have a Next.js app. What should preflight check before I start validation? What would cause a CLEAR vs BLOCKED verdict?
test_prompts.expected_output: Should enumerate: Node.js installed, npm installed, node_modules exists, dev server port available, .env.local configured. CLEAR if all pass. BLOCKED if Node missing or major port conflict. Should explain: 'preflight runs first, 5-10 min, saves 30 min of wasted validation time on missing dependencies.'

test_prompts.prompt: My Django app's database is not running. Preflight detects the failure. Should it auto-start Postgres or report BLOCKED?
test_prompts.expected_output: Should say: 'Preflight attempts auto-fix: docker run postgres (or equivalent), waits, re-checks. If it succeeds, CLEAR. If docker not found, report BLOCKED — user must install Docker manually. Never auto-install Docker (major tool).'

test_prompts.prompt: I'm setting up a fullstack app (React + Node + MongoDB). In what order should preflight run checks?
test_prompts.expected_output: Should explain bottom-up: 1) MongoDB running? 2) Node server running and /api/health responds? 3) React dev server running? 4) Can browser hit frontend? If any layer below fails, skip layers above. Report verdict per layer.
